stem: A decision-maker must choose between two options. Option A (horizontal scaling) yields a guaranteed return of $92K. Option B (vertical scaling) depends on traffic load patterns: with probability 36-54% (uncertain), it yields $162K; otherwise it yields $15K. Compute the expected value of each option and determine which is better. How does the uncertainty in traffic load patterns affect the recommendation?

ground_truth.answer: EV(A) = $92K (fixed). EV(B) ranges from $67.9K to $94.4K. Point estimate: $81.2K. Option A is preferred at most assumptions (EV_A > EV_B when P(good) < 52.4%).

solution_steps: The decision switches at P(good) = 52.4%, which falls within the uncertainty range.
common_failure_modes: Picking a single probability and giving a definitive recommendation without sensitivity analysis
Not computing the breakeven probability where the optimal decision switches
Ignoring the range of outcomes and focusing only on expected values (risk-neutral assumption)
